title: Weyl Node
summary: A Weyl node is an isolated three-dimensional two-band crossing whose linearized Hamiltonian is a Weyl Hamiltonian and whose local topological charge is nonzero.
assumptions: The crossing is isolated in three-dimensional momentum space.
A two-band local reduction is valid near the node.
regime: Low-energy expansion near an isolated 3D crossing.
scope: Lecture One treatment of local two-band crossings and their topological charge.

source_anchors.notes: The local 2x2 Hamiltonian determines the Weyl cone and its handedness.

representation: Local 2x2 Bloch Hamiltonian with linearized Pauli-matrix coefficients.

math_expressions.notes: Generic local 2x2 Hamiltonian near the crossing.
Local chirality of the Weyl node.

failure_modes: If the determinant of the linearization vanishes, the simple Weyl description can fail.
Global lattice consistency is not fixed by the local cone alone.
retrieval_hints: Primary entry point for Weyl semimetal queries.
